name: absorption-grade large-N extension of the A=0.5 corner
description: Extends the absorption-grade A=0.5 collapse-time sweep to N=256, 512, 1024 to test whether the 3.2x reduced-to-finite-N prolongation (and its N-independence) survives asymptotically. The published primary sweep covered N=4..64 (16x range); this adds another 16x (N=64..1024). Same absorption criterion, same dynamics core, same model/breath knobs, same seeds (seed0=100000) as absorption.config.json's primary sweep, so every large-N point is paired seed-for-seed with the published small-N points and uses the identical t_abs definition. New output file only; absorption.config.json and all prior results are untouched.

model._note: Identical to absorption.config.json so t_abs is measured under the exact same criterion and cadence; t_max=2000s is far above the observed ~130s lifetimes (zero censoring at A=0.5 for N<=64, and the large-N pilot stays in the same band).
